source_jobs.title: Technical Palantir Foundry & AIP Instructor - DC
source_jobs.description:
ARE YOU AN ENGINEER WHO LOVES TO TEACH AND KNOWS PALANTIR FOUNDRY & AIP?

Our team of instructors and engineers is enabling the next generation of Palantir experts. We help Palantir customers & partners build the in-house expertise they need to build AI-enabled solutions self-sufficiently. We are an official Palantir Partner and the sole Palantir Training & Assessment Partner.

We are seeking a senior full-time instructor to deliver live, recorded, and written content.




BENEFITS

 - In-person in a NOVA/DC/MD office at least 3d/wk

 - $200k-240k salary depending on experience + profit sharing

 - Health/Dental/Vision

 - 401(k) matching

 - Ample PTO

 - Target start date: mid-April




WORK SUMMARY

 - Creating and recording live content for YouTube and internal video offerings. We will rent and furnish a dedicated studio space for you to record professional quality tutorials.

 - Building and enhancing existing technical Foundry curriculum.

 - Delivering in-person and virtual live, multi-day Foundry trainings




NATURE OF THE COMPANY

 - Work intensely when you’re at work; don’t work when you’re not at work

 - Turn Slack and email notifications off

 - Take pride in your work

 - Direct, candid, and respectful feedback

 - We flex on scope, not deadlines à la Shape Up https://basecamp.com/shapeup and other writing from 37Signals/Basecamp

 - We have small offices in California, Oklahoma, and New York (you will be the first person in a DC office)




EXPERIENCE

 - Requirements:
   
   - You must love teaching! If you're on the fence about teaching, read no further.
   
   - Strong technical skills: Python, TS/JS, using LLMs in data pipelines and applications, Spark, containerization, unit and integration testing, observability & monitoring
     
     - In other words, at many companies you would fit in as a developer
   
   - Be one of the following:
     
     - Former Palantir FDE or technical data strategist (we're looking for a "Delta" or a very strong "Techo")
     
     - Foundry user with 3+ years experience delivering 2-3 enterprise use cases
     
     - 10+ years experience in a"data-driven" industry across business domains with experience delivering end-to-end technical solutions from use case design to technical implementation

 - Nice to have:
   
   - Full-stack development skills (e.g. an app powered by React + FastAPI + Postgres -- but we're not picky about the tech stack)
   
   - Machine Learning experience
   
   - Foreign language skills, especially Japanese, Korean, German, Spanish




ADDITIONAL SKILLS

 - Effective written and oral communication skills
   
   - You write and speak clearly
   
   - You can identify what matters for a non-technical user and succinctly explain what they need to know in terms that make sense to them
     
     - e.g., an executive who needs to make a decision based on your input
   
   - You proactively communicate about what you’re doing — this is habitual for you
   
   - You can disagree and navigate conflict respectfully and in a manner that puts the team’s goals first

 - High degree of autonomy (i.e., don’t wait for work to come to you)

 - You're always learning new things. What was the last new technology you learned?




TRAVEL

This role will require domestic and international travel for in-person trainings and periodic internal company events.

An in-person training typically lasts four days at the customer's office. This role is expected to cover in-person trainings primarily in the broader DC area, but occasional travel may be required to other places.

source_jobs.title: Technical Content & Curriculum Developer - Palantir Foundry & AIP
source_jobs.description:
Ontologize is the sole Palantir Training & Assessment Partner. We help Palantir customers and partners build the in-house expertise they need to develop and operate AI-enabled solutions independently.

We're hiring a Technical Curriculum & Content Developer to build and improve the learning content that powers our Foundry and AIP trainings. This is a curriculum-first role where you will take complex platform workflows and turn them into clear, structured learning experiences — labs, exercises, walkthroughs, assessments, and reference material for both technical and business-facing learners.

You will work closely with our instructors and directly shape what they deliver. This role is not primarily about leading live sessions, but about making sure the underlying curriculum is technically strong, well sequenced, and useful for learners. You should be willing to critique learning design, not just produce documents: if a module is technically accurate but hard to learn from, your job is to make it better. Over time, strong performers who are interested in teaching may grow into delivery responsibilities, but that is a future path, not a day-one expectation.

You would be joining a small, distributed company with teammates across the US, Paris, and Rio de Janeiro, working from our Bengaluru office in Whitefield. The expectation for this role in 3+ days in office.

 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build technical curriculum for Foundry and AIP — design labs, exercises, and walkthroughs that reflect real enterprise use cases, not toy examples

 - Write clear explanations and reference material for audiences ranging from experienced developers to business analysts configuring their first pipeline

 - Maintain and improve existing content — keep curriculum current as the platform evolves, and refine for clarity, pacing, and technical accuracy based on learner feedback

 - Support learners asynchronously — answer questions, debug lab issues, and identify recurring friction points that reveal gaps in the curriculum

 - Work closely with instructors to improve delivery materials and close the gap between what learners struggle with and what the content actually teaches

 - Design assessments and rubrics that measure whether learners are building real capability, not just completing modules

 


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You love making complex things teachable. If you don't get a kick out of finding the cleanest way to land a tricky concept, this role won't feel right.

 - You have meaningful Palantir Foundry & AIP experience. This comes from hands-on Foundry work implementing end-to-end use cases over 3+ years, delivering enterprise outcomes on the platform.

 - You have a strong technical foundation. You should be comfortable with Python, TypeScript, Spark, LLM-enabled workflows, data engineering fundamentals, and basic software engineering practices. You should be comfortable reading and writing production-quality code even though this is not a developer role.

 - You write clearly and precisely. You can take ambiguous technical material and make it teachable i.e. simplifying without dumbing it down.

 - You have good judgment about what learners actually need. You understand the difference between what is technically complete and what is genuinely useful for someone trying to learn.

 - You think carefully about how technical people learn. You can structure concepts, examples, and exercises in a way that helps learners progress rather than just absorb information.

 - You are proactive and autonomous. You communicate what you are working on before anyone has to ask, and you do not wait for tasks to be assigned.

 


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience building technical curriculum, documentation, labs, or certification content

 - Full-stack development experience (any stack — we care about the thinking, not the framework)

 - Machine learning experience

 - Foreign language skills (English is a must; anything else is a bonus)

 


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS

notes: Palantir-focused training and assessment partner with a strong engineering core
